title: Cash Hurts to Spend — That Pain Is Worth $31,000
hook: Paying with cash literally activates your brain's pain center — and that discomfort could be the most underrated money hack alive.
angle: The 'pain of paying' is neuroscience, not willpower — cash users spend 30% less than card users, translating to real retirement wealth most people ignore.
audience: 25-45 year olds who overspend on cards and think cash is inconvenient
key_numbers: 30% — how much less cash users spend vs. card users (Drazen Prelec / MIT study)
$31,000 — what a 30% spending cut saves over 10 years at 7% invested
100ms — how quickly your brain's insula fires pain signals when handing over cash
2x — how much more people will pay for the same item when using a card
93% — share of US transactions now cashless, even as spending anxiety rises
